name: cartesian_line_descent

symptoms: object is swept/raked sideways during the approach-to-grasp descent (moved several cm before the fingers close)
fingers close at the object's ORIGINAL location after the object has been nudged away
trace_evidence: object XY drifts during the descent phase (e.g. 58 mm) while finger/palm <-> object contacts flicker
trajectory = joint-space interpolation between only TWO IK solutions (pre-grasp, grasp) — the TCP path is an arc, not a line

when_to_apply: Any time the end-effector must move in a straight Cartesian line through clutter (final grasp descent, peg insertion, shelf reach) but the controller interpolates in joint space.
repair_strategy: Discretize the Cartesian line into waypoints (8-10), solve IK at each waypoint seeded from the previous one, and joint-interp only BETWEEN CONSECUTIVE waypoints. The TCP then tracks the line to within the waypoint spacing.
code_sketch: w = solve_arm_ik_full_pose(env, grasp_pt - d*s, d, jaw, seed=seed)
for q0, q1 in zip(qs[:-1], qs[1:]):
for q in interp(q0, q1, V_DESCEND): step(act(q))
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro rubiks-cube grasp (object drift 58 mm -> ~5 mm)
